LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE GRANADA, INC., MICHAEL LIEBERMAN AND IRENE LIEBERMAN'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. SUSTAIN DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD A REPRESENTATION OF A PAST OR EXISTING FACT, OR THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT INTEND TO PERFORM THE PROMISES MADE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
ORDER OVERRULING IN PART AND SUSTAINING IN PART DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
ORDER ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE GRANADA, INC., MICHAEL LIEBERMAN AND IRENE LIEBERMAN'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 11, 2007. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT COURTESY COPIES AND COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, EFENDANT THE GRANADA, INC., MICHAEL LIEBERMAN AND IRENE LIEBERMAN'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT: DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS (FINAL) LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS, SPECIFICALLY IN PARAGRAPHS EIGHT AND TWENTY-FIVE, THAT ARE SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A BASIS THAT RICHARD LIEBERMAN HAD NO INTENTION OF PERFORMING THE PROMISES ALLEGEDLY MADE. TARMANN V STATE FARM (1991) 2 CAL. APP. 4TH, 153, 158. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE UNDER TARMANN, THE CAUSE OF ACTION CANNOT BE PREMISED ON PREDICTIONS OR PROMISES AS TO FUTURE EVENTS. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-11-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE GRANADA, INC., MICHAEL LIEBERMAN AND IRENE LIEBERMAN'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT: DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION.
APPEARANCES: SONYA SMALLETS, ESQUIRE (LAWLESS & LAWLESS, 415.391.7555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS. CAROL C. COPSEY, ESQUIRE (GORDON & REES, 415.986.5900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS (FINAL) LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS, SPECIFICALLY IN PARAGRAPHS EIGHT AND TWENTY-FIVE, THAT ARE SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A BASIS THAT RICHARD LIEBERMAN HAD NO INTENTION OF PERFORMING THE PROMISES ALLEGEDLY MADE. TARMANN V STATE FARM (1991) 2 CAL. APP. 4TH, 153, 158. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE UNDER TARMANN, THE CAUSE OF ACTION CANNOT BE PREMISED ON PREDICTIONS OR PROMISES AS TO FUTURE EVENTS. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas To David Cohen, M.D., Peter Hui, M.D. And Fastrack
Off Calendar Dec-06-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. SONYA SMALLETS APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

After hearing the matter, the tentaive ruling is adoped. Plaintiff Stanley Zukowski's motion to quash subpoenas is granted in part. The subpoenas are modified to only require production of medical records relating to loss of appetite, nausea, insomnia and emotional distress, including but not limited to feelings of insecurity, low self-esteem, anxiety, humiliation, embarrassment, and anger. The motion to quash the subpoena as to Fastrak records is granted.
Plaintiff to submit proposed order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
